AML.T0051.name: LLM Prompt Injection
AML.T0051.description: An adversary crafts inputs (direct or indirect) that override the model's instructions to make it take unintended actions through its tools.

AML.T0054.name: LLM Jailbreak
AML.T0054.description: An adversary bypasses the model's safety guardrails so it performs restricted operations (e.g. shell/command execution) via its tools.

AML.T0057.name: LLM Data Leakage
AML.T0057.description: An adversary induces the model to disclose sensitive data (secrets, PII, proprietary content) through tool calls, including encoded/obfuscated exfiltration.

AML.T0053.name: LLM Plugin Compromise
AML.T0053.description: An adversary abuses a plugin/tool the agent can call to gain capabilities beyond intended scope (excessive agency, unsafe tool use).

AML.T0050.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
AML.T0050.description: An adversary executes commands or scripts through an agent tool (shell/interpreter) to run arbitrary code.

AML.T0049.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
AML.T0049.description: An adversary exploits an internet-facing application the agent exposes (e.g. SQL injection through a tool) to gain access or manipulate data.

AML.T0012.name: Valid Accounts
AML.T0012.description: An adversary uses legitimate credentials/identity to access resources across tenants; enforced by cross-tenant/namespace isolation at the tool boundary.

AML.T0055.name: Unsecured Credentials
AML.T0055.description: An adversary harvests credentials/secrets an agent can reach through its tools. Enforced by blocking secret-reading tool calls (get_secret, read_env, read_secret and similar).

AML.T0056.name: LLM Meta Prompt Extraction
AML.T0056.description: An adversary extracts the system/meta prompt via crafted inputs. Extraction happens in model I/O, outside the tool-call boundary, so no runtime tool-call rule can detect it — control it with system-prompt hardening and SDK output-DLP.

AML.T0061.name: LLM Prompt Self-Replication
AML.T0061.description: A self-replicating prompt propagates through tool outputs to other agents/systems. Propagation rides tool OUTPUTS while the runtime PEP governs tool CALLS — control it with SDK output-DLP and downstream input validation.

AML.T0024.name: Exfiltration via ML Inference API
AML.T0024.description: Membership inference / model inversion / extraction performed against the model's inference API. Occurs below the tool-call boundary — a runtime tool-call PEP cannot observe raw inference.

AML.T0048.name: External Harms
AML.T0048.description: Financial/reputational/societal/user harm resulting from an AI system. An impact/governance concern, not a tool-call vector a runtime PEP enforces directly.

AML.T0020.name: Poison Training Data
AML.T0020.description: An adversary poisons the training dataset. A build/training-time supply-chain concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.

AML.T0018.name: Backdoor ML Model
AML.T0018.description: An adversary embeds a backdoor in the model during training. A model-lifecycle concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.

AML.T0031.name: Erode ML Model Integrity
AML.T0031.description: An adversary degrades the model's integrity/accuracy over time. A model-integrity/monitoring concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.
